text:
There once was a wise old owl who lived in a great big oak tree in the forest.

One winter day, a young squirrel named Charlie wanted to prove itself worthy of a place in the wise owl's tree.

Charlie would climb high among the barks, searching for acorns all day long.

One day as Charlie was descending, a big black bear swooped down and snatched a hefty acorn right out of Charlie's hand.

The young squirrel hurried up the tree searching for another.

Eventually, Charlie found an old oak tree filled with acorns just like that one.

The owl saw Charlie hoarding the acorns and glared down at the little squirrel.

"Young one," the owl hissed. "Take just enough so you'll find your way back up every day."

So Charlie took just one more acorn than he caught, sitting on a branch all day staring into the owl's eyes.

Finally evening fell and Charlie flew down feeling full-hearted.

The wise owl chomped
